_description: Clause Interaction Engine — defines hard dependencies, soft suggestions, and conflicts between clauses. Used by the IRE to validate document completeness and logical consistency.

hard_dependencies.rationale: A contract with a fixed term must specify how it can be terminated before expiry — without termination provisions the parties have no exit mechanism.
Termination clauses are ineffective without a valid notice mechanism. Notice of termination must be served in a manner that provides legal evidence of receipt.
Unlimited indemnity creates open-ended liability. Indian courts award only reasonable compensation (ICA S.73/74). A liability cap defines the upper limit.
Default clause without acceleration right is commercially toothless — the lender cannot call the loan early.
hard_dependencies.law: Indian Contract Act, 1872 S.39; IBC, 2016 S.7

hard_dependencies.rationale: Acceleration without defined Events of Default is void for uncertainty — courts will not enforce acceleration without specified triggering events.
Confidentiality obligation is unenforceable without a defined scope of what constitutes Confidential Information.
Post-employment non-compete is void under ICA S.27 unless narrowly tied to protection of specific Confidential Information (Niranjan Shankar Golikari SC).
Employment termination without a defined notice period violates IDA 1947 and applicable Shops Acts. Summary dismissal without notice valid only for gross misconduct.
EPF and ESI are mandatory statutory deductions. A wage clause without addressing statutory deductions creates compliance risk and potential criminal liability.
Dispute resolution clause specifying seat of arbitration implies Indian law governs. Both must be consistent.
Cannot have a signature block without identifying who is signing and their authority.
IP assignment without consideration may be challenged. A deed of assignment without consideration is not a contract — it requires deed formalities.
Force majeure protection is triggered by notice. Without a notice mechanism, the affected party has no clear way to invoke FM protection.
Leases exceeding 12 months must be registered under Registration Act 1908 S.17. Unregistered leases are inadmissible as evidence.

soft_suggestions.reason: Modern confidentiality obligations should specify technical and organisational security measures — important for DPDPA 2023 compliance.
Indemnity obligations should be backed by insurance coverage to ensure the indemnifying party can actually pay claims.
Service scope without SLA creates uncertainty about performance standards — disputes about what 'reasonable skill and care' means.
Scope creep is the most common dispute in service contracts. A change order mechanism prevents disputes about out-of-scope work.
Software IP agreements should address open source licence compliance to prevent GPL contamination.
Natural justice requires an employee to be heard. A grievance mechanism balances the disciplinary process.

conflict_rules.reason: Contradictory — independent contractor relationship clause is inconsistent with employment wages and statutory benefits clauses.
conflict_rules.resolution: Determine whether the relationship is employment or independent contractor. Use employment clauses for employees; use service/scope/payment clauses for contractors.

conflict_rules.issue: Post-employment non-compete without nexus to Confidential Information
conflict_rules.reason: Absolute post-employment non-compete is void under ICA S.27.
conflict_rules.resolution: Limit post-employment restriction to: (1) protection of specific Confidential Information; (2) reasonable duration (max 12 months); (3) named clients with whom employee had personal dealings.

conflict_rules.issue: Exclusive jurisdiction clause without arbitration exception
conflict_rules.reason: Absolute exclusive jurisdiction clause ousts all other courts — void under ICA S.28.
conflict_rules.resolution: Frame exclusive jurisdiction clause as subsidiary to arbitration clause, or as applicable only where no arbitration agreement exists.

conflict_rules.issue: Foreign governing law chosen between two Indian residents
conflict_rules.reason: May be void under ICA S.23 as against public policy; Indian courts will apply Indian law regardless.
conflict_rules.resolution: Use Indian governing law for agreements between Indian parties. Foreign law may be chosen for international commercial agreements.
